q1.text: What is the primary role of a goalkeeper?
q1.explanation: The goalkeeper's main job is to prevent the opposing team from scoring.

q2.text: Which part of the body can a goalkeeper use inside the penalty area that outfield players cannot?
q2.explanation: Goalkeepers can use their hands within their own penalty area.

q3.text: What color jersey does a goalkeeper typically wear?
choices: A different color from all other players
q3.explanation: Goalkeepers must wear a distinguishing color from teammates, opponents, and referees.

q4.text: Which legendary Italian goalkeeper is known as 'Superman'?
q4.explanation: Gianluigi Buffon earned the nickname 'Superman' for his extraordinary saves.

q5.text: Which goalkeeper has won the most World Cup Golden Glove awards?
q5.explanation: Manuel Neuer won the Golden Glove at the 2014 World Cup.

q6.text: What is the 6-yard box also known as?
q6.explanation: The 6-yard box is officially called the goal area.

q7.text: Which goalkeeper scored the most career goals?
q7.explanation: Rogério Ceni scored over 130 goals in his career, mostly from free kicks and penalties.

q8.text: Who was the first goalkeeper to win the Ballon d'Or?
q8.explanation: Lev Yashin won the Ballon d'Or in 1963, the only goalkeeper ever to do so.

q9.text: What famous save technique involves diving with a scorpion-like kick behind the back?
q9.explanation: René Higuita popularized the scorpion kick save in 1995 against England.

q10.text: Can a goalkeeper handle a deliberate back-pass from a teammate?
choices: No, it results in an indirect free kick
Yes, but only in extra time
Only if the ball crosses the 6-yard box
q10.explanation: The back-pass rule (1992) prohibits goalkeepers from handling deliberate passes from teammates.

q11.text: Which goalkeeper captained Spain to their 2010 World Cup victory?
q11.explanation: Iker Casillas was Spain's captain during their triumphant 2010 World Cup campaign.

q12.text: What is the standard number of defenders in a 4-3-3 formation?

choices: A match where no fouls were committed
A match where the team did not concede a goal
A match with no yellow cards
q13.explanation: A clean sheet means the team conceded zero goals in the match.

q14.text: Which defender position plays in the center of the back line?
q14.explanation: Center backs play in the middle of the defensive line.

choices: A defensive strategy where the line pushes up to catch attackers offside
q15.explanation: The offside trap involves defenders stepping forward in unison before a pass to catch attackers offside.

q16.text: Which Spanish defender holds the record for most international caps for Spain?
q16.explanation: Sergio Ramos holds Spain's record with 180 international caps.

choices: A defender who plays behind the main defensive line
q17.explanation: The sweeper plays behind the defensive line and 'sweeps up' any balls that get past the main defenders.

q18.text: Which Italian defensive system is famous for its tight man-marking?
q18.explanation: Catenaccio is an Italian defensive tactic emphasizing tight man-marking and a sweeper.

q19.text: Which defender won the 2006 Ballon d'Or?
q19.explanation: Fabio Cannavaro won the 2006 Ballon d'Or after captaining Italy to the World Cup title.

q20.text: Which defender has scored the most goals in Champions League history?
q20.explanation: Sergio Ramos holds the record for most goals by a defender in Champions League history.

q21.text: Who is the youngest player to captain a Champions League winning team?
q21.explanation: Paolo Maldini captained AC Milan in Champions League victories at a remarkably young age.

choices: An attempt to take the ball from an opponent
q22.explanation: A tackle is the act of challenging an opponent to win possession of the ball.

q23.text: Which country is Paolo Maldini from?
q23.explanation: Paolo Maldini is Italian and spent his entire career at AC Milan.

q24.text: Which defender has made the most appearances in World Cup history?
q24.explanation: Lothar Matthäus holds the record for most World Cup appearances with 25 matches.

q25.text: In what situation is a penalty kick awarded?
choices: Foul inside the penalty area by the defending team
q25.explanation: A penalty kick is awarded when the defending team commits a foul inside their own penalty area.

choices: A foul by the last defender that denies a clear goal-scoring opportunity
q26.explanation: A last-man foul denies an obvious goal-scoring opportunity and typically results in a red card.

q27.text: Which left-back is famous for his powerful free kicks and played for Real Madrid and Brazil?
q27.explanation: Roberto Carlos was known for his incredible left-foot free kicks and played for Real Madrid from 1996 to 2007.

q28.text: How many defenders typically form a back three?
q28.explanation: A back three uses 3 center-backs, often with wing-backs providing width.

q29.text: What does 'marking' mean in defensive play?
choices: Staying close to an opponent to prevent them receiving the ball
q29.explanation: Marking means closely guarding an opponent to prevent them from receiving or playing the ball effectively.

q30.text: Which defender played 647 consecutive league matches for AC Milan without missing a game?
q30.explanation: Franco Baresi was renowned for his consistency and longevity at AC Milan.

q31.text: Which nation did Franz Beckenbauer represent?
q31.explanation: Franz Beckenbauer represented West Germany and is considered one of the greatest defenders ever.

q32.text: What is the main role of a central midfielder?
choices: Link defense and attack, control the tempo
q32.explanation: Central midfielders connect defense to attack and dictate the pace of the game.

choices: How many goals a team scores
The percentage of time a team has the ball
q33.explanation: Possession refers to the percentage of time a team controls the ball during a match.

q34.text: Which country invented 'tiki-taka' style of play?
q34.explanation: Spain and FC Barcelona are credited with developing the tiki-taka passing style.

q35.text: Which midfielder is known as 'The Architect' and played for Barcelona and Spain?
q35.explanation: Xavi Hernández was nicknamed 'The Architect' for his ability to orchestrate play.

choices: A midfielder who covers the full length of the pitch, defending and attacking
q36.explanation: A box-to-box midfielder contributes at both ends of the pitch, covering penalty area to penalty area.

q37.text: Which French midfielder won 3 consecutive Ballon d'Or awards in the 1980s?
q37.explanation: Michel Platini won the Ballon d'Or in 1983, 1984, and 1985.

q38.text: Who scored the famous volley goal in the 2002 Champions League final?
q38.explanation: Zinedine Zidane scored a stunning left-foot volley in the 2002 Champions League final against Bayer Leverkusen.

q39.text: Which midfielder has the most assists in Champions League history?
q39.explanation: Lionel Messi holds the record for most assists in Champions League history.

choices: A pass played into space behind the defense for a teammate to run onto
q40.explanation: A through ball splits the defense and allows an attacker to run onto it in a goal-scoring position.

q41.text: Which German midfielder captained Bayern Munich and Germany, known for his versatility?
q41.explanation: Bastian Schweinsteiger captained both Bayern Munich and Germany and could play multiple positions.

q42.text: Who holds the record for most La Liga assists of all time?
q42.explanation: Lionel Messi holds the record for the most assists in La Liga history.

choices: A defensive midfielder who shields the back line
q43.explanation: The holding midfielder sits in front of the defense, breaking up opponent attacks and distributing the ball.

q44.text: Which midfielder scored in two different World Cup finals?
q44.explanation: Zinedine Zidane scored in the 1998 World Cup final (2 goals) and the 2006 final (1 goal via penalty).

choices: Passing the ball to a teammate
Running with the ball at your feet while maintaining control
q45.explanation: Dribbling is the skill of moving the ball forward while keeping it under close control.

q46.text: Which midfielder scored the winning goal in the 2010 World Cup final?
q46.explanation: Andrés Iniesta scored the winning goal in extra time against Netherlands in the 2010 World Cup final.

q47.explanation: A hat-trick is when a player scores three goals in a single match.

q48.text: Which player has scored the most goals in FIFA World Cup history?
q48.explanation: Miroslav Klose scored 16 goals across four World Cups for Germany.

choices: Award for the top scorer in a tournament
q49.explanation: The Golden Boot is awarded to the top scorer in a football tournament.

q50.text: Who won the most Ballon d'Or awards?
q50.explanation: Lionel Messi has won the Ballon d'Or 8 times, more than any other player.

q51.text: Which striker scored 91 goals in a calendar year (2012)?
q51.explanation: Lionel Messi scored 91 goals in 2012, breaking Gerd Müller's record of 85 goals in 1972.

choices: A winger who plays as a center back
A center forward who drops deep into midfield to create space
A defender who acts as a striker
q52.explanation: The False 9 drops deep from the striker position to link play and create space for teammates.

q53.text: Who scored the 'Hand of God' goal?
q53.explanation: Diego Maradona scored the 'Hand of God' goal against England in the 1986 World Cup quarter-final.

q54.text: Which Brazilian striker won the World Cup twice (1994 and 2002)?
q54.explanation: Ronaldo Nazário won the World Cup in 1994 (as a squad member) and 2002 (as the star striker).

choices: Kicking the ball while it is in the air before it bounces
q55.explanation: A volley is striking the ball while it is still in the air.

q56.text: Which club did Erling Haaland join from Borussia Dortmund?
q56.explanation: Erling Haaland transferred from Borussia Dortmund to Manchester City in 2022.

q57.text: Who is the all-time top scorer in Champions League history?
q57.explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo holds the record for the most goals in Champions League history.

q58.text: Which country did the legendary striker Pelé represent?
q58.explanation: Pelé played for Brazil and won three FIFA World Cups (1958, 1962, 1970).

q59.text: Which striker scored the fastest hat-trick in Premier League history?
q59.explanation: Sadio Mané scored the fastest Premier League hat-trick in 2 minutes 56 seconds for Southampton against Aston Villa in 2015.

q60.text: What does 'offside' mean for an attacker?
choices: Being behind the ball when it is passed
Being nearer to the opponent's goal line than the second-last defender when the ball is played
q60.explanation: An attacker is offside if they are nearer to the goal line than both the ball and the second-last defender.

q61.text: Which World Cup saw the most goals scored in a single tournament by one player (13 goals)?
q61.explanation: Just Fontaine scored 13 goals at the 1958 World Cup, a record that still stands.

q62.text: Which defender has won the most Champions League titles?
q62.explanation: Marcelo won 5 Champions League titles with Real Madrid.

q63.text: Which midfielder has scored the most goals in World Cup finals history?
q63.explanation: Pelé scored in multiple World Cup finals, accumulating the most final goals.

choices: Using the head to clear the ball away from danger in the defensive area
q64.explanation: A header clearance is when a defender uses their head to direct the ball away from their goal.

q71.text: Which French legend presented the 2022 Ballon d'Or to Karim Benzema?
q71.explanation: Zinedine Zidane presented the 2022 Ballon d'Or to Karim Benzema.

q72.text: Who scored the fastest goal in FIFA World Cup history?
q72.explanation: Hakan Şükür scored after 11 seconds for Turkey against South Korea in 2002.

q73.text: Who scored the winning goal in the 2010 FIFA World Cup final?
q73.explanation: Andrés Iniesta scored in extra time against the Netherlands.

q74.text: Who is Real Madrid's all-time top scorer?
q74.explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo is Real Madrid's all-time leading scorer.

q75.text: Which Italian defender captained Italy to the 2006 World Cup and won the Ballon d'Or that same year?
q75.explanation: Fabio Cannavaro captained Italy in 2006 and won the Ballon d'Or.

q76.text: Who assisted Andrés Iniesta's winning goal in the 2010 World Cup final?
q76.explanation: Cesc Fàbregas provided the assist for Iniesta's goal.

q77.text: Which goalkeeper won the Golden Ball at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
q77.explanation: Oliver Kahn won the Golden Ball in 2002 after outstanding performances for Germany.

q78.text: Which Brazilian striker was nicknamed 'O Fenômeno'?
q78.explanation: Ronaldo Nazário was famously known as 'O Fenômeno'.

q79.text: Which defender scored Real Madrid's dramatic equalizer in the 2014 Champions League final against Atlético Madrid?
q79.explanation: Sergio Ramos scored the famous 93rd-minute equalizer in Lisbon.

q80.text: Which player won the 2007 Ballon d'Or?
q80.explanation: Kaká won the Ballon d'Or in 2007 after starring for AC Milan.

q81.text: Which country did Hakan Şükür represent internationally?
q81.explanation: Hakan Şükür was one of Turkey's most famous strikers.

q82.text: Which goalkeeper became famous for the 'scorpion kick' save?
q82.explanation: René Higuita made the scorpion kick save famous in 1995.

q83.text: Which Croatian midfielder won the Ballon d'Or in 2018?
q83.explanation: Luka Modrić ended the Messi-Ronaldo streak by winning the 2018 Ballon d'Or.

q84.text: Who scored the winning goal in the 2014 FIFA World Cup final?
q84.explanation: Mario Götze scored in extra time against Argentina.

q85.text: Which legendary AC Milan defender was known as 'Il Capitano'?
q85.explanation: Paolo Maldini is one of the greatest defenders in football history.

q86.text: Who is the only goalkeeper to have won the Ballon d'Or?
q86.explanation: Lev Yashin won the Ballon d'Or in 1963.

q87.text: Who scored the famous bicycle kick goal for Real Madrid against Juventus in the Champions League in 2018?
q87.explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo scored one of the most famous bicycle kicks in Champions League history.

q88.text: Which Spanish midfielder was known for controlling games with short passing and vision, and played for Barcelona?
q88.explanation: Xavi was one of the masters of possession football with Barcelona and Spain.

q89.text: Which national team did Carles Puyol represent?
q89.explanation: Carles Puyol was a key defender for Spain and won the 2010 World Cup.

q90.text: Which player scored 13 goals in a single FIFA World Cup tournament, a record that still stands?
q90.explanation: Just Fontaine scored 13 goals at the 1958 World Cup.

q91.text: How wide is a regulation football goal?
q91.explanation: A standard football goal is 7.32 metres (8 yards) wide and 2.44 metres tall.

q92.text: Which goalkeeper won the Premier League Golden Glove a record 5 times?
q92.explanation: Pepe Reina won the Premier League Golden Glove award five times with Liverpool.

q93.text: Which goalkeeper conceded the fewest goals in a single Premier League season (38 games)?
q93.explanation: Petr Čech conceded only 15 goals in the 2004–05 Premier League season for Chelsea.

q94.text: What is the maximum number of steps a goalkeeper can take while holding the ball?
q94.explanation: A goalkeeper can take a maximum of 4 steps while holding the ball before they must release it.

q95.text: Which goalkeeper famously saved three penalties in a 2005 Champions League final shootout?
q95.explanation: Jerzy Dudek made three penalty saves for Liverpool against AC Milan in Istanbul 2005.

q96.text: Which national team did legendary goalkeeper Lev Yashin play for?
q96.explanation: Lev Yashin, known as the 'Black Spider', was the Soviet Union's legendary goalkeeper.

q97.text: Which goalkeeper holds the record for the most clean sheets in Premier League history?
q97.explanation: Petr Čech holds the Premier League record with 202 clean sheets.

q98.text: From where is a goal kick taken?
q98.explanation: A goal kick can be taken from anywhere inside the goal area (the 6-yard box).

q99.text: Which goalkeeper scored a competitive goal directly from a drop kick?
q99.explanation: Alisson Becker scored a dramatic header for Liverpool against West Brom in May 2021.

q100.text: Which World Cup saw Germany goalkeeper Oliver Kahn win the Golden Ball award despite finishing as runners-up?
q100.explanation: Oliver Kahn won the Golden Ball at the 2002 World Cup despite Germany losing the final to Brazil.

q101.text: Which goalkeeper kept a clean sheet in a World Cup final and never conceded in 1,142 minutes of international football?
q101.explanation: Dino Zoff went 1,142 minutes without conceding for Italy and captained them to the 1982 World Cup.

q102.text: What colour card results in a player being immediately sent off?
q102.explanation: A red card means the player is immediately sent off and cannot be replaced.

q103.text: Which Brazilian right-back won the Champions League with both Barcelona and Juventus?
q103.explanation: Dani Alves won the Champions League with Barcelona and later won Serie A and Coppa Italia with Juventus.

q104.text: Who is the only defender to win the FIFA World Player of the Year award?
q104.explanation: Fabio Cannavaro won the FIFA World Player of the Year in 2006 after Italy's World Cup triumph.

q105.text: What restart takes place after the ball goes out of play over the touchline?
q105.explanation: A throw-in is awarded when the ball crosses the touchline.

q106.text: Which English defender captained Chelsea to back-to-back Premier League titles in 2005 and 2006?
q106.explanation: John Terry captained Chelsea through their historic back-to-back league titles under José Mourinho.

q107.text: Which defender was known as 'The Kaiser' and won the World Cup as both player and manager?
q107.explanation: Franz Beckenbauer won the World Cup in 1974 as a player and in 1990 as Germany's manager.

q108.text: Which nation does Virgil van Dijk represent internationally?
q108.explanation: Virgil van Dijk captains the Netherlands national team.

choices: Each defender man-marks a specific opponent
Defenders guard zones of the pitch rather than specific players
q109.explanation: In zonal marking, defenders cover assigned areas of the pitch instead of following individual opponents.

q110.text: Which English club went an entire 49-game unbeaten league run between 2003 and 2004?
q110.explanation: Arsenal's 'Invincibles' went 49 Premier League games unbeaten under Arsène Wenger.

q111.text: Which Portuguese defender plays for Manchester City and is known for his leadership at the back?
q111.explanation: Rúben Dias won the Premier League with Manchester City and was named the league's best player in 2020–21.

q112.text: Which Dutch defender captained his country to the 1988 European Championship?
q112.explanation: Ruud Gullit captained the Netherlands to their only major international trophy at Euro 1988.

q113.text: How many players are on each side in a standard football match?
q113.explanation: Each team fields 11 players, including the goalkeeper, in a standard match.

q114.text: Which Spanish defender scored the winning goal for Barcelona in the 2009 Champions League final?
q114.explanation: Gerard Piqué scored Barcelona's second goal in the 2009 Champions League final win over Manchester United.

q115.text: Which left-back won 5 Champions League titles and 9 La Liga titles, all with Real Madrid?
q115.explanation: Marcelo won 5 Champions League titles with Real Madrid, making him the most decorated player in the competition.

q116.text: What trophy is awarded to the best player at each FIFA World Cup?
q116.explanation: The Golden Ball is awarded to the best player of the FIFA World Cup tournament.

q117.text: Which Brazilian midfielder is famous for his dribbling, tricks, and playing for FC Barcelona?
q117.explanation: Ronaldinho dazzled the world with his tricks and flair during his time at FC Barcelona.

q118.text: Which midfielder won the Champions League with three different clubs?
q118.explanation: Clarence Seedorf won the Champions League with Ajax (1995), Real Madrid (1998), and AC Milan (2003, 2007).

q119.text: What does 'pressing' mean in modern football?
choices: Aggressively chasing the ball high up the pitch to win it back quickly
Playing a long ball over the defense
q119.explanation: Pressing means pressuring the opponent immediately after losing the ball to win it back quickly.

q120.text: Which German midfielder was nicknamed 'Der Bomber der Nation' for his prolific scoring?
q120.explanation: Gerd Müller was nicknamed 'Der Bomber' for his incredible goal-scoring instinct for Bayern Munich and Germany.

q121.text: Which club did Kevin De Bruyne join before moving to Manchester City?
q121.explanation: Kevin De Bruyne starred for VfL Wolfsburg, winning the Bundesliga Player of the Season, before joining Man City.

q122.text: Which midfielder is known as 'La Pulga' (The Flea) due to his small stature?
q122.explanation: Lionel Messi is nicknamed 'La Pulga' because of his small frame and quick, elusive movement.

q123.text: What is a 'free kick' in football?
choices: A kick awarded to the opposing team after a foul
A kick taken without any defenders nearby
A kick awarded to restart the game after a goal
q123.explanation: A free kick is awarded to a team after the opponents commit a foul.

q124.text: Which midfielder scored the winning goal in extra time of the 1998 World Cup final?
q124.explanation: Emmanuel Petit scored the third goal in France's 3–0 win over Brazil in the 1998 World Cup final.

q125.text: Which Italian playmaker was famous for his vision and passing at Juventus in the 1980s?
q125.explanation: Michel Platini was the dominant force in Italian football at Juventus, winning three Serie A titles.

q126.text: Who scored the 'Ghost Goal' for England against Germany in the 2010 World Cup?
q126.explanation: Frank Lampard's shot clearly crossed the line against Germany but was not awarded in the 2010 World Cup.

choices: A kick awarded when the ball goes out over the byline last touched by the attacking team
A kick awarded when the ball goes out over the byline last touched by the defending team
A restart after the ball goes out of play over the touchline
A free kick from the edge of the box
q127.explanation: A corner kick is awarded to the attacking team when the defending team last touches the ball over their own byline.

q128.text: Which midfielder was named the best player at the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
q128.explanation: Luka Modrić won the Golden Ball at the 2018 World Cup after inspiring Croatia to the final.

q129.text: Which Spanish midfielder is the all-time leading assist provider in Champions League history at his retirement?
q129.explanation: Xavi Hernández made a record 21 assists in the Champions League during his career with Barcelona.

q130.text: Which club's stadium is known as 'The Theatre of Dreams'?
q130.explanation: Old Trafford, home of Manchester United, is nicknamed 'The Theatre of Dreams'.

q131.text: What is the penalty spot distance from the goal line?
q131.explanation: The penalty spot is exactly 11 metres (12 yards) from the goal line.

q132.text: Which striker scored a hat-trick in the 2022 World Cup final but still ended up on the losing side?
q132.explanation: Kylian Mbappé scored a hat-trick in the 2022 World Cup final but France lost to Argentina on penalties.

q133.text: Which Dutch striker scored 35 goals in a single Bundesliga season in 2022?
q133.explanation: Erling Haaland scored 35 Bundesliga goals in the 2021–22 season for Borussia Dortmund.

choices: A pass or action that directly leads to a goal being scored
A shot that hits the post
q134.explanation: An assist is credited to the player whose pass or action directly leads to a goal.

q135.text: Which French striker was the top scorer at Euro 2000?
q135.explanation: Patrick Kluivert of the Netherlands finished as joint top scorer at Euro 2000 with 5 goals.

q136.text: Who scored a famous backheel goal for Juventus against Internazionale in the 2015 Coppa Italia semi-final?
q136.explanation: Carlos Tévez scored an outrageous backheel against Inter Milan in the 2015 Coppa Italia.

q137.text: Which country does Karim Benzema represent internationally?
q137.explanation: Karim Benzema plays for France, despite having Algerian heritage.

q138.text: Which striker holds the record for most goals in a single Premier League season with 34 goals?
q138.explanation: Andrew Cole scored 34 Premier League goals for Newcastle United in the 1993–94 season.

q139.text: Which Argentine striker was sold by Barcelona for a world record fee to PSG in 2017?
q139.explanation: Neymar Jr. transferred from Barcelona to PSG in 2017 for a world record €222 million fee.

q140.text: Which Italian striker wore the number 9 shirt for Italy and won the 2006 World Cup?
q140.explanation: Luca Toni wore the number 9 shirt and was Italy's main striker at the 2006 World Cup.

q141.text: Which English striker is the all-time Premier League top scorer?
q141.explanation: Alan Shearer scored 260 Premier League goals, a record that still stands.

q142.text: Who scored the goal that eliminated France from Euro 2004 with a last-minute strike?
q142.explanation: Angelos Charisteas scored the only goal as Greece shocked France in the Euro 2004 quarter-final.

q143.text: What nationality is striker Robert Lewandowski?
q143.explanation: Robert Lewandowski is Polish and has been one of Europe's top scorers for over a decade.

q144.text: Which striker scored in 11 consecutive Champions League matches, a record?
q144.explanation: Ruud van Nistelrooy scored in 11 consecutive Champions League games for Manchester United in 2002–03.

q145.text: Which Argentine goalkeeper won the 2022 FIFA World Cup with his country?
q145.explanation: Emiliano Martínez was crucial in Argentina's 2022 World Cup triumph, making key saves in the semi-final and final shootouts.

q146.text: What does the acronym 'VAR' stand for?
q146.explanation: VAR stands for Video Assistant Referee, a system introduced to assist match officials.

q147.text: How long is a standard football match (excluding extra time)?
q147.explanation: A standard football match lasts 90 minutes, divided into two 45-minute halves.

q148.text: Which country won the first ever FIFA World Cup in 1930?
q148.explanation: Uruguay hosted and won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930, defeating Argentina 4–2 in the final.

q149.text: Which goalkeeper went unbeaten for 13 consecutive World Cup matches, a record?
q149.explanation: Peter Shilton went a record 13 World Cup matches without conceding a goal for England.

q150.text: Which defender has won the most Champions League titles as a player?
q150.explanation: Marcelo won 5 Champions League titles with Real Madrid between 2013 and 2022.

q151.text: Inside which area is a goalkeeper allowed to handle the ball?
q151.explanation: A goalkeeper is only allowed to use their hands inside their own penalty area.

q152.text: Which Italian goalkeeper won the 2006 FIFA World Cup with Italy?
q152.explanation: Gianluigi Buffon was Italy's starting goalkeeper in their 2006 World Cup-winning campaign.

q153.text: Which Brazilian goalkeeper captained Brazil in the 1970 World Cup final?
q153.explanation: Félix was Brazil's goalkeeper and captain in the 1970 World Cup final against Italy.

choices: The line in the middle of the pitch
The horizontal bar joining the two goalposts
The line of the penalty area
q154.explanation: The crossbar is the horizontal bar at the top of the goal.

q155.text: Which goalkeeper was England's number one at the 1966 World Cup and later made the famous 'Banks of England' save?
q155.explanation: Gordon Banks is one of England's greatest goalkeepers and is remembered for his save from Pelé in 1970.

q156.text: Which goalkeeper became the first ever winner of the Yashin Trophy in 2019?
q156.explanation: Alisson Becker won the first Yashin Trophy, the award created for the world's best goalkeeper.

q157.text: What is a goalkeeper's main duty during a penalty kick?
choices: Stop the ball from entering the goal
q157.explanation: The goalkeeper's job during a penalty is to try to save the shot.

q158.text: Which goalkeeper started for France in the 2018 World Cup final?
q158.explanation: Hugo Lloris captained France and started in their 2018 World Cup final victory.

q159.text: Which goalkeeper scored more than 100 career goals, mostly from penalties and free kicks?
q159.explanation: Rogério Ceni is the highest-scoring goalkeeper in football history.

q160.text: Which Spanish goalkeeper was Barcelona's regular starter during much of Pep Guardiola's era?
q160.explanation: Víctor Valdés was Barcelona's first-choice goalkeeper during their dominant Guardiola years.

q161.text: What is a rebound save situation?
choices: When the ball comes back into play after a save
When the goalkeeper takes a throw-in
When the ball hits the corner flag
q161.explanation: A rebound happens when the goalkeeper saves the ball but it remains in play.

q162.text: Which goalkeeper won the Golden Glove at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
q162.explanation: Manuel Neuer won the Golden Glove after an outstanding tournament for Germany in 2014.

q163.text: What is the main job of a center-back?
choices: Protect the area in front of goal and stop attackers
q163.explanation: Center-backs are primarily responsible for defending central areas and stopping attacks.

q164.text: Which defender captained Barcelona in the 2011 Champions League final?
q164.explanation: Carles Puyol was Barcelona's captain during the 2011 Champions League final.

q165.text: Which German defender scored in the 2014 World Cup final after coming on as a substitute?
q165.explanation: Mario Götze scored the extra-time winner in the 2014 World Cup final.

q166.text: What does a defender do when they 'block' a shot?
choices: Stop the shot from reaching goal
Kick it for a throw-in automatically
q166.explanation: Blocking a shot means getting in the way to prevent the ball from reaching the goal.

q167.text: Which legendary Italian defender wore number 3 for AC Milan for most of his career?
q167.explanation: Paolo Maldini famously wore the number 3 shirt for AC Milan.

q168.text: Which defender won Euro 2000 and the 1998 World Cup with France and later coached France to the 2018 World Cup title?
q168.explanation: Didier Deschamps won major trophies as a player and later coached France to the 2018 World Cup.

choices: A wide defender with both defensive and attacking duties
A striker who drifts to the wing
A center-back who never leaves the box
q169.explanation: A wing-back plays on the flank and contributes both defensively and offensively.

q170.text: Which Spanish defender scored the winning goal in the 2010 World Cup semi-final against Germany?
q170.explanation: Carles Puyol scored with a header to send Spain into the 2010 World Cup final.

q171.text: Which defender is known as 'Der Kaiser'?
q171.explanation: Franz Beckenbauer was nicknamed 'Der Kaiser' and is one of the greatest defenders ever.

choices: A goal scored with the head
A defender using their head to remove danger
q172.explanation: A headed clearance is when a defender uses their head to clear the ball away from danger.

q173.text: Which Brazilian defender is famous for his powerful free kicks and long-range shots from left-back?
q173.explanation: Roberto Carlos became famous for his incredible left foot and spectacular free kicks.

q174.text: Which Dutch defender scored twice for the Netherlands in the Euro 1988 final?
q174.explanation: Ruud Gullit scored in the Euro 1988 final as the Netherlands beat the Soviet Union.

q175.text: What does it mean when defenders 'hold the line'?
choices: They all stay deep inside the box
They keep their shape together across the pitch
q175.explanation: Holding the line means defenders stay coordinated and maintain the defensive shape together.

q176.text: Which Italian defender was captain of Juventus and part of Italy's 2006 World Cup-winning squad?
q176.explanation: Giorgio Chiellini became one of Italy and Juventus's great defensive leaders.

q177.text: What is the job of a defensive midfielder?
choices: Protect the defense and recover the ball
q177.explanation: A defensive midfielder shields the back line and helps win the ball back.

q178.text: Which Spanish midfielder scored the winning goal in the 2010 FIFA World Cup final?
q178.explanation: Andrés Iniesta scored in extra time to give Spain their first World Cup title.

q179.text: Which Dutch midfielder scored one of the most famous volleys in history in the Euro 1988 final?
q179.explanation: Marco van Basten scored an iconic volley in the Euro 1988 final against the Soviet Union.

q180.text: What is a long ball in football?
choices: A high or driven pass played over a long distance
q180.explanation: A long ball is a pass played over a significant distance, often to switch play or find a forward quickly.

q181.explanation: Andrés Iniesta was affectionately nicknamed 'Don Andrés' because of his class and elegance.

q182.text: Which midfielder won the Ballon d'Or in 2007 after starring for AC Milan?
q182.explanation: Kaká won the 2007 Ballon d'Or after a brilliant year for AC Milan.

q183.text: What is a pass completion rate?
choices: The number of goals from passes
The percentage of passes that successfully reach a teammate
The number of long balls attempted
q183.explanation: Pass completion rate measures how many of a player's passes are accurate.

q184.text: Which Croatian midfielder won the Ballon d'Or in 2018?
q184.explanation: Luka Modrić won the 2018 Ballon d'Or after a brilliant year for club and country.

q185.text: Which midfielder won the Champions League with Ajax, Real Madrid, and AC Milan?
q185.explanation: Clarence Seedorf is famous for winning the Champions League with three different clubs.

q186.text: What does a midfielder do when they 'dictate the tempo'?
choices: Control the speed and rhythm of the game
q186.explanation: Dictating the tempo means controlling how quickly or slowly the team plays.

q187.text: Which French midfielder won three consecutive Ballon d'Or awards in the 1980s?
q187.explanation: Michel Platini won the Ballon d'Or in 1983, 1984, and 1985.

q188.text: Which midfielder scored Germany's winning goal in the 2014 World Cup final assist move before the finish?
q188.explanation: André Schürrle provided the cross for Mario Götze's winning goal in the final.

q189.text: What is an interception in midfield?
choices: Stopping an opponent's pass before it reaches its target
q189.explanation: An interception is when a player reads the pass and cuts it out.

q190.text: Which English midfielder was known for wearing number 8 at Chelsea and scoring many goals from midfield?
q190.explanation: Frank Lampard became one of the highest-scoring midfielders in Premier League history.

q191.text: What is a striker's main job?
choices: Score goals and lead the attack
q191.explanation: A striker's primary responsibility is to score goals and create danger in attack.

q192.text: Which Portuguese forward is Real Madrid's all-time top scorer?
q192.explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo is Real Madrid's all-time leading scorer.

q193.text: Which striker scored twice for Brazil in the 2002 World Cup final?
q193.explanation: Ronaldo Nazário scored both goals in Brazil's 2-0 win over Germany in the 2002 final.

q194.text: What is a header in football?
choices: Kicking the ball in the air
Using the head to play the ball
q194.explanation: A header is when a player uses their head to direct the ball.

q195.text: Which French striker scored the golden goal in the Euro 2000 final?
q195.explanation: David Trezeguet scored the golden goal that won Euro 2000 for France.

q196.text: Which striker scored four goals for Real Madrid in the 2017 Champions League final era campaign final season run and is the all-time top scorer in the competition?
q196.explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo is the all-time top scorer in Champions League history and was central to Real Madrid's 2017 triumph.

choices: A shot taken with the knee
q197.explanation: A bicycle kick is an overhead acrobatic attempt at goal.

q198.text: Which Norwegian striker joined Manchester City in 2022?
q198.explanation: Erling Haaland joined Manchester City in 2022.

q199.text: Which player scored the 'Hand of God' goal in the 1986 World Cup?
q199.explanation: Diego Maradona scored the famous 'Hand of God' goal against England.

choices: A shot aimed toward the post closest to the attacker
A shot from the halfway line
A volley from outside the box
q200.explanation: A near-post finish is aimed at the side of the goal nearest to the shooter.

q201.text: Which striker is Argentina's all-time top scorer?
q201.explanation: Lionel Messi is Argentina's all-time top scorer.

q202.text: Which striker scored the fastest hat-trick in Premier League history?
q202.explanation: Sadio Mané scored the fastest Premier League hat-trick in 2 minutes 56 seconds.

q203.text: What does a forward do when making a 'run in behind'?
choices: Sprint into space behind the defense
Stay wide near the corner flag only
Move back to their own penalty area
q203.explanation: A run in behind is made to attack the space behind the defensive line.

q204.text: Which Uruguayan striker is nicknamed 'El Pistolero'?
q204.explanation: Luis Suárez is famously known as 'El Pistolero'.

q205.text: Which French striker won the 1991 Ballon d'Or while playing for Marseille?
q205.explanation: Jean-Pierre Papin won the 1991 Ballon d'Or after his brilliant performances for Marseille.

choices: A shot taken without controlling the ball first
The first shot of the game
A penalty taken in the first half
A shot from the center circle
q206.explanation: A first-time finish is struck immediately without taking a touch to control the ball.

q207.text: Which striker scored the winning goal for Spain in the Euro 2008 final?
q207.explanation: Fernando Torres scored the only goal in Spain's Euro 2008 final win over Germany.

q208.text: Which striker scored in both the 1958 and 1970 World Cup finals for Brazil?
q208.explanation: Pelé scored in the 1958 and 1970 World Cup finals for Brazil.

q209.text: Which player won the Golden Boot at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
q209.explanation: James Rodríguez finished as the top scorer of the 2014 World Cup with 6 goals.

q210.text: Which forward scored the winning goal for Manchester United in the 1999 Champions League final?
q210.explanation: Ole Gunnar Solskjær scored the dramatic stoppage-time winner against Bayern Munich.
